title: Fumana glutinosa
intro: Fumana glutinosa, also known as sticky wallflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Fumana glutinosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Fumana glutinosa is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Fumana glutinosa is classified within the family Cistaceae. It belongs to the genus Fumana. The species is a type of annual or biennial herb. Fumana glutinosa is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Western Asia. It is also known as sticky-leaved rockrose or sticky-leaved fumana.
